def insert_safe(data: list, element: any, index: int) -> list:
    if index < 0 or index > len(data):
        return data
    data.insert(index, element)
    return data

class CustomList:
    MAX_CAPACITY = 10

    def __init__(self):
        self.items: list[int] = []

    def add(self, item: int) -> bool:
        """
        Adds an item to the custom list, checking against maximum capacity.

        Args:
            item: The integer value to be added.

        Returns:
            True if the item was successfully added, False otherwise.
        """
        if len(self.items) < self.MAX_CAPACITY:
            self.items.append(item)
            return True
        return False

    def get_items(self) -> list[int]:
        """
        Returns the current list of items.

        Returns:
            A list containing all items currently in the list.
        """
        return self.items

import time

def benchmark_append(data_list: list, item: object) -> float:
    start_time = time.perf_counter()
    data_list.append(item)
    end_time = time.perf_counter()
    return end_time - start_time

def benchmark_insert(data_list: list, index: int, item: object) -> float:
    start_time = time.perf_counter()
    data_list.insert(index, item)
    end_time = time.perf_counter()
    return end_time - start_time

def append_tuple_to_list(data_list: list, new_tuple: tuple) -> list:
    data_list.append(new_tuple)
    return data_list

if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_list = [10, "hello", 3.14]
    new_tuple = (True, 42, 99.9)
    result = append_tuple_to_list(input_list, new_tuple)
    print(result)
